methodology: Multi-strategy gap analysis v4 (Rust, ontology-integrated). Each claimed gap is tested with (1) narrow PubMed query [Title/Abstract], (2) broad PubMed query with full synonym expansion [Title/Abstract], (3) Europe PMC independent search, (4) local corpus scan of landscape abstracts. Gene synonyms are auto-generated from NCBI ontology annotations (8283 genes across 8 pathogens) and merged with manual synonyms. Only claims with CONFIRMED confidence (0 in all strategies) are reported as gaps. UNCERTAIN and FALSE claims are flagged for review.

summary.confirmed_unique_gene_targets_note: 8 query-level gap entries deduplicate to 6 biological genes: J2R and thymidine_kinase are OPG101 (same gene, two nomenclatures); A56R and hemagglutinin are OPG185 (same gene, two nomenclatures). See paper Section 3.4 footnote.
summary.confirmed_gene_gaps_note: 8 query-level gaps (J2R+thymidine kinase and A56R+hemagglutinin are 2 names each for the same Mpox gene). deduplicated = 6 gene-level gaps (6 unique biological gene targets (5 Mpox + 1 cholera)).
